title: allionia lanceolata subsp. uniflora
intro: Allionia lanceolata subsp. uniflora is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Nyctaginaceae family. It is also known as Allionia uniflora or four o'clock and has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties. The plant is known to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and soothing digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Allionia lanceolata subsp. uniflora is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically to the skin in the form of creams or ointments.
